localizations.title: 7 Wonders
localizations.hint_101: The resources shown on this card can be found elsewhere.
localizations.hint_94_first: Use your notes to determine which columns should be inserted above each of the gods.
localizations.hint_94: Carefully examine the depictions of the gods you've seen during your adventure, as well as the laurel wreaths on the game box.
localizations.hint_94_part2: First, scan the roof. Then, search for the Deer constellation in your notes.
localizations.hint_78: To determine the Rhodes card number, rely on the priestess’ notes and the numbers associated with the wonders of Olympia and Halicarnassus.
localizations.hint_21_first: You have only discarded one item since your adventure started.
localizations.hint_21: Use the resources shown on the item you have discarded during this adventure.
localizations.hint_47: The same symbols are used to represent the military strength of Olympia, Halicarnassus, and Rhodes.
localizations.hint_66: You received gold coins in Giza that you haven't used yet.
localizations.hint_69: Insert this card into its designated spot to complete the frame.
localizations.hint_26: Insert this card into its designated spot to complete the frame.
localizations.hint_75: Insert this card into its designated spot to complete the frame.
localizations.hint_machine_75: Open the correct valves that will allow you to water all the plants, without watering the same plant twice or otherwise wasting water.
localizations.hint_90_first: You found compasses during your adventure, and can also find references to morning glory and purslane plants somewhere.
localizations.hint_90: Each of the plants (morning glory and purslane) is linked to a number somewhere in the Architect's Guild. This information can help you identify which plants are which in the diagram.
localizations.hint_68: Insert this card into its designated spot to complete the frame.
localizations.hint_74: The goatherd explains how you should position yourself in front of the three pyramids.
localizations.hint_33_first: Compare the features on the Colossus of Rhodes Wonder with those on the Statue of Zeus at Olympia to find something in common between them, proving the navigator wrong.
localizations.hint_33: The common feature is not illustrated on the Wonders themselves, but finding the feature on the cards will allow you to take a new card.
localizations.hint_44: Adjust the card and use your device to illuminate the lighthouse.
localizations.hint_8: When you come across these symbols, you have all the information necessary to properly translate each symbol.
localizations.hint_89: After placing the stones in the gaps between the crates, they will form a 3D pair of two digits.
localizations.hint_70_first: One of the people assisting you has provided advice for what to do when you are attacked.
localizations.hint_70: According to the navigator (51), you must invert the positions of the two halves of your crew. Invert the two parts of a particular element on card 12, then combine that newly formed element with card 70.
localizations.hint_212: The architects mention a specific distance that you should sail.
localizations.hint_0: Though it may not seem intuitive, you can combine this card with a red card.
localizations.hint_48: You need to find the 9 cards whose weights match the specified values.
localizations.hint_23_first: The list of cities provided by Pythagoras (14) is partially obscured by another of his notes.
localizations.hint_23: Pay attention to the first letter of each city in order to trace a route to Halicarnassus.
localizations.hint_41: You must find something to light your way.
localizations.hint_39: To begin the restoration work, choose which stone to place first.
localizations.hint_91: There is something you can give to this goatherd.
localizations.hint_1_first: Use the map to recreate the scene described.
localizations.hint_1: Place the ship (12) on the map at the center of the Mediterranean Sea. Then look for a set of resources.
localizations.hint_67: Examine the cards on the map to follow the merchant's route in order to figure out the number of your next card.
localizations.hint_53: Use the navigator's advice in order to lave the port (20) with your ship (12).
localizations.hint_27: To determine the Rhodes card number, rely on the priestess’ notes and the numbers associated with the wonders of Olympia and Halicarnassus.
localizations.hint_59: If you buy all the tools from this merchant, you'll receive two of each, when you only need one of each.
localizations.hint_34: Insert this card into its designated spot to complete the frame.
localizations.answer_hint_101: The resources in this frame (wood, wood, wood, cloth) are the same as those found on the back of card 12.
localizations.answer_hint_94: Overlap cards 31, 83, and 95 to recreate the sequence of symbols found on card 8. The symbol for Poseidon is visible on card 53; insert cards 49 and 81 to form the Fish constellation above the statue of Poseidon. The symbol for Zeus is found on card 68; place cards 9 and 25 to form the Swan constellation above the statue of Zeus. The symbol for Helios is visible on card 34; place cards 16 and 64 to form the Bow constellation above the statue of Helios. The symbol for Athena can be seen on card 8; place cards 4 and 36 to form the Scales constellation above the statue of Athena. Enter the symbols found above each column with a laurel wreath into the app.
localizations.answer_hint_94_part2: Connect the stars to create the Deer constellation; this deer is depicted on card 95.
localizations.answer_hint_78: According to the Priestess, Rhodes is equidistant from the cities of Halicarnassus (26) and Olympia (68). The number exactly halfway between 26 and 68 is 47.
localizations.answer_hint_21: You have only discarded one card since the start of this adventure: Card 23. The back of this card depicts these resources: ore, stone, gold, papyrus. Card 31 has these same resources on its back.
localizations.answer_hint_47: The military strengths of Rhodes, Halicarnassus, and Olympia are symbolized by a red icon with 2 crossed swords. Rhodes has 8 of these symbols, Olympia has 2, and Halicarnassus has 3. The difference in strength between Rhodes and Olympia is 6 (8 - 2), while the difference in strength between Rhodes and Halicarnassus is 5 (8 - 3). Put these digits together to get 65.
localizations.answer_hint_66: By placing card 11 on the map. You have obtained 4 gold coins. However, there are no cards that feature 4 coins. However, the merchant will allow you to exchange your gold coins for stone. Therefore, you can trade the 4 coins to obtain 4 stone. Only one card has 4 stone resources on its back - card 89.
localizations.answer_hint_69: Insert this card into its designated spot on the map (the one labeled The Lighthouse of Alexandria). This will complete the frame, which depicts these resources: glass, glass, glass, wood; you can take the card with these 4 resources on its back.
localizations.answer_hint_26: Insert this card into its designated spot on the map (the one labeled The Mausoleum of Halicarnassus). This will complete the frame, which depicts these resources: wood, wood, stone, cloth; you can take the card with these 4 resources on its back.
localizations.answer_hint_75: Insert this card into its designated spot on the map (the one labeled The Hanging Gardens of Babylon). This will complete the frame, which depicts these resources: papyrus, cloth, brick, stone; you can take the card with these 4 resources on its back.
localizations.answer_hint_machine_75: To water each plant without wasting any water, press the central valve 4 times, the top left valve once, the bottom left valve once, the top right valve twice, and the bottom right valve once.
localizations.answer_hint_90: On the board found in the Architect's Guild (84), you can read "Morning Glory 2" and "Purslane 1". These represent the number of roots each plant has, which corresponds to the number of lines coming out of each shoot in the diagram (90). Place your compasses (50) so that its two points indicate the two shoots highest up on the card that correspond to these plants. The arrangement of the compasses coupled with the diagram will form the number 8.
localizations.answer_hint_68: Insert this card into its designated spot on the map (the one labeled The Statue of Zeus at Olympia). This will complete the frame, which depicts these resources: cloth, glass, cloth, stone; you can take the card with these 4 resources on its back.
localizations.answer_hint_74: Follow the goatherd's directions to reach the market. Access Machine 11 and align the three pyramids so that their apexes meet at a single point.
localizations.answer_hint_33: The priestess indicates that someone in Rhodes is asserting something untrue (78). The Wonders of Olympia and Rhodes each have the same resources printed on them: glass and stone. Take the card showing these resources: glass, glass, stone, stone.
localizations.answer_hint_44: Place the light source against the back of card 44 so that the light shines through the top of the lighthouse. You will see the number 69 illuminated.
localizations.answer_hint_8: Use the symbols found on cards 14, 78, 53, and 8 to associate each resource with a symbol visible in Machine 75.
localizations.answer_hint_89: If you fit the stone blocks into the empty spots in the hold, they will form the number 3 on one side and 7 on the other.
localizations.answer_hint_70: According to the navigator (51), you must invert the positions of the two halves of your crew. To do this, invert the two parts of a particular element on card 12: the card number itself. Inverting each of these parts will thus form the number 21. Combine this with card 70. 70 + 21 = 91.
localizations.answer_hint_212: The architects (C) say that you must travel 31 nautical miles. Add that distance to your ship (12). 31 + 12 = 43
localizations.answer_hint_0: The sculptor (0) can duplicate any engraving. You can give him your tablet so he can replicate the existing part to replace the missing part. You have two cards numbered 55 in your deck. Not to worry! 55 + 0 = 55.
localizations.answer_hint_48: The architects inform you that you need nine blocks, each with a weight that is a perfect square. Therefore, take the cards from the deck that correspond to the perfect square numbers: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, and 81.
localizations.answer_hint_23: Pythagoras (14) has given you a list of the the cities you must visit. The first letters of each of these cities spell out "Halicarnassus". Connect the cities of Hapasa, Attuda, Labranda, Idyina, Caunos, Astyra, Razilli, Nisyros, Alinda, Sina, Samos, Ula, and finally Smyrna; this route forms the number 26.
localizations.answer_hint_41: You can see a red puzzle piece on the back of card 41. Add the number of that card to the brazier (3) to find your way. 41 + 3 = 44
localizations.answer_hint_39: The begin the work of restoring the temple, first place the engraving of Artemis (55) as the centerpiece stone of the temple (39). 39 + 55 = 94
localizations.answer_hint_91: Give the goat (7) to the goatherd (+67). 7 + 67 = 74
localizations.answer_hint_1: Place the ship (12) on the map so that it is in the middle of the Mediterranean Sea. Once placed, the four corners of the card will point to these resources: wood, cloth, cloth, wood.
localizations.answer_hint_67: The merchant says he always follows a particular route; first he goes to Olympia (68), then to Alexandria (69). He then goes to Giza, which he says is "merely one step beyond Alexandria," meaning that you should add 1 to the card number for Alexandria 69 + 1 = 70.
localizations.answer_hint_53: The navigator's note refers to "the strength of the waves". Count the number of waves on the map, then add that number (9) to those of your ship (12) and the port you are leaving (20). 9 + 12 + 20 = 41
localizations.answer_hint_27: According to the Priestess, Rhodes is equidistant from the cities of Halicarnassus (26) and Olympia (68). The number exactly halfway between 26 and 68 is 47.
localizations.answer_hint_59: To determine the price of only 1 set of each of the 3 tools, add up the total cost of everything the merchant sells: 100. This is the cost of 2 complete sets of tools, so if you only want 1 of each tool, you will only pay half that price (50).
localizations.answer_hint_34: Insert this card into its designated spot on the map (the one labeled The Colossus of Rhodes). This will complete the frame, which depicts these resources: glass, stone, stone, wood; you can take the card with these 4 resources on its back.
localizations.machine_11: When you position yourself properly, you can see the market from a distance and analyze the alignment of the pyramids.
localizations.machine_44_light: We were unable to turn on the flashlight feature on your device. Please use another light source instead.
localizations.machine_94_step_1: Before you begin, remove the lid from the box, and set the bottom part of the box nearby.
localizations.machine_94_step_2: Leave the columns you placed inside the box; then flip the map over, and place the map on top of the columns.
localizations.end: The Temple of Artemis has been rebuilt. Blessed by the goddess herself, this edifice has reclaimed its place among the 7 Wonders of the Ancient World, and will inspire philosophers for generations: "I have gazed upon the walls of magnificent Babylon, where chariots can race, the Zeus by the banks of the Alpheus, the Hanging Gardens, the Colossus of Helios, the massive works of the great pyramids, the opulent tomb of Mausolus; but when I saw the house of Artemis rising to the heavens, all else was eclipsed, and I said: 'Apart from the Olympian Zeus, there is nothing that has ever been seen like this!'"
localizations.intro: Take the map from the box and unfold it.
